### 支付宝外国用户反馈 

1月10日

.lily….

本人已使用护照完成实名认证，符合平台要求的合法成年用户。但在实际使用过程中发现，关键支付功能（如向个人账户转账）被强制要求绑定中国大陆银行卡。对于非中国籍用户而言，拥有中国银行卡本身并非可行或普遍条件。这一要求在产品逻辑上与“支持护照实名认证”“面向海外用户开放注册”的宣传存在明显矛盾。如果平台在核心功能上仍以中国居民身份与中国银行卡体系作为唯一前提，那么当前对外国用户开放注册与实名认证的做法，实际上只提供了形式上的可用性，而非真实可使用的服务体验。希望平台能够明确区分“可注册”与“可使用”的边界，或对护照实名认证用户提供真正可落地的支付解决方案，而非将无法满足的条件转嫁给用户本身。

### 五福活动让人失望 

2025/02/02

jjj365

关于支付宝五福活动的投诉我深感对支付宝今年的五福活动感到失望与不满。作为支付宝的长期用户，我一直期待着每年的五福活动，然而今年的活动却让我大失所望。首先，我觉得活动的公平性有待提高。在收集福字的过程中，我发现某些福字的获取难度极高，甚至需要依赖特定的渠道或关系才能获得，这无疑增加了活动的不公平性。其次，活动的奖励机制也存在问题。尽管我费尽心力地收集齐了五福，但最终获得的奖励却与我的付出严重不符，这让我感到非常沮丧。我希望支付宝能够认真对待用户的反馈，对五福活动进行改进和优化。希望未来的活动能够更加公平、透明，让每一个用户都能享受到活动的乐趣和实惠。同时，也希望支付宝能够加强对活动规则的监管，确保活动的公正性和合理性

**支付宝**，是[蚂蚁金服](https://wuu.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%9A%82%E8%9A%81%E9%87%91%E6%9C%8D "蚂蚁金服")旗下个第三方支付平台，2004年12月由[中国](https://wuu.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E4%B8%AD%E5%9B%BD "中国")[阿里巴巴集团](https://wuu.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E9%98%BF%E9%87%8C%E5%B7%B4%E5%B7%B4%E9%9B%86%E5%9B%A2 "阿里巴巴集团")勒[杭州](https://wuu.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E6%9D%AD%E5%B7%9E "杭州")创办，乃朝属于浙江蚂蚁金服。支付宝可以进行在线支付，官方应用支持信用卡免费还款，话费Q币充值、水电燃气费缴款。

取自“[https://wuu.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=支付宝&oldid=378260](https://wuu.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E6%94%AF%E4%BB%98%E5%AE%9D&oldid=378260)”

data.title: Alipay+ | Connecting Global Brands with Mobile Consumers
data.description: Alipay+ supports global mobile payment solutions, serving millions of businesses of all sizes. Consumers can pay like locals anywhere using their preferred payment methods, such as e-wallets.
data.content:

The unified gateway for the world's leading payment methods

The unified wallet gateway for cross-border payment and digital services, connecting global merchants with billions of mobile users worldwide

2

B+

Consumers

50

Mobile payment providers

10

+

National switches

220+

Markets

150

M+

QR merchants

150

M+

NFC merchants

One integration. Global interoperability.

Alipay+ is the unified wallet gateway, connecting global merchants to a wide variety of payment methods used by billions of mobile users through a single integration. Consumers can pay using their preferred method, merchants can engage more customers with AI‑powered digital solutions and tailored rewards, and mobile payment providers can harness our advanced wallet tech to build the next generation of super apps — creating a seamless, innovative ecosystem for all.

![Image 5: Brand Title](https://cdn.marmot-cloud.com/storage/intl_website/2026/04/24/n9sCeer/tagline.png)

data.title: Alipay
data.content:
Alipay[![Image 1](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/d/dd/Alipay_logo_%282024%29.svg/120px-Alipay_logo_%282024%29.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Alipay_logo_(2024).svg)
Native name 支付宝
[Romanized](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Romanization "Romanization") name Zhīfùbǎo
Industry[Financial services](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Financial_services "Financial services")

[Payment processor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Payment_processor "Payment processor")
Founded February 2004;22 years ago in [Hangzhou](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hangzhou "Hangzhou"), Zhejiang, China
Founder[Jack Ma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jack_Ma "Jack Ma")
Headquarters[Pudong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pudong "Pudong"), 
Shanghai

, 
China
Area served Worldwide
Products[Electronic payment processing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E-commerce_payment_system "E-commerce payment system")

[Banking](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bank "Bank")

[Mobile payment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mobile_payment "Mobile payment")
[Parent](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parent_company "Parent company")[Ant Financial](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ant_Financial "Ant Financial")
Website[www.alipay.com](https://www.alipay.com/)

| Alipay |
| --- |
| [Traditional Chinese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters") | 支付寶 |
| [Simplified Chinese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters") | 支付宝 |
| | Transcriptions | | --- | | [Standard Mandarin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standard_Chinese "Standard Chinese") | | [Hanyu Pinyin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hanyu_Pinyin "Hanyu Pinyin") | Zhīfùbǎo | |

**Alipay** ([simplified Chinese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"):支付宝; [traditional Chinese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"):支付寶; [pinyin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"):_zhīfùbǎo_) is a third-party [mobile](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mobile_payments_in_China "Mobile payments in China") and [online payment platform](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Payment_service_provider "Payment service provider"), established in [Hangzhou](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hangzhou "Hangzhou"), China, in February 2004 by [Alibaba Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alibaba_Group "Alibaba Group") and its founder [Jack Ma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jack_Ma "Jack Ma"). In 2015, Alipay moved its headquarters to [Pudong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pudong "Pudong"), Shanghai, although its parent company [Ant Financial](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ant_Financial "Ant Financial") remains Hangzhou-based.[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-1)

Alipay overtook [PayPal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PayPal "PayPal") as the world's largest mobile (digital) payment platform in 2013.[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:12-2) As of June 2020, Alipay serves over 1.3 billion users and 80 million merchants.[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:0-3) According to the statistics of the fourth quarter of 2018, Alipay has a 55.32% share of the third-party payment market in mainland China, and it continues to grow.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-4)[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-5)[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-6)

Along with [WeChat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WeChat "WeChat"), Alipay has been described to be China's [super-app](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Super-app "Super-app") with a wide range of functionalities including [ridesharing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ridesharing_company "Ridesharing company"), travel booking and medical appointments.[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-7)[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-8)

The service was first launched in 2003, by [Taobao](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taobao "Taobao").[[9]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-9)[[10]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-10)[[11]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-11) The [People's Bank of China](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/People's_Bank_of_China "People's Bank of China"), China's central bank, issued licensing regulations in June 2010 for third-party payment providers. It also issued separate guidelines for foreign-funded payment institutions.[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-12) Because of this, Alipay, which accounted for half of China's non-bank online payment market[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation\_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_], was restructured as a domestic company controlled by Alibaba [CEO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CEO "CEO")[Jack Ma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jack_Ma "Jack Ma") in order to facilitate the regulatory approval for the license.[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-13) The 2010 transfer of Alipay's ownership was controversial, with media reports in 2011 that [Yahoo!](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yahoo! "Yahoo!") and [Softbank](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Softbank "Softbank") ([Alibaba Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alibaba_Group "Alibaba Group")'s controlling shareholders) were not informed of the sale for nominal value. Chinese business publication _[Century Weekly](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caixin\_Media "Caixin Media")_ criticised Ma, who stated that Alibaba Group's board of directors was aware of the transaction.[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-14) The incident was criticised in foreign and Chinese media as harming foreign trust in making Chinese investments.[[15]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-15) The ownership dispute was resolved by [Alibaba Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alibaba_Group "Alibaba Group"), [Yahoo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yahoo! "Yahoo!")!, and [Softbank](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SoftBank_Group "SoftBank Group") in July 2011.[[16]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-16)

In 2013, Alipay launched a financial product platform called [Yu'e Bao](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yu'e_Bao?action=edit&redlink=1 "Yu'e Bao (page does not exist)")[[zh](https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E4%BD%99%E9%A2%9D%E5%AE%9D "zh:余额宝")].[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:1-17) Alipay partnered with [Tianhong Asset Management](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tianhong_Asset_Management "Tianhong Asset Management") to launch the it.[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:1-17) Yu'e Bao offers an online money market account in which Alipay customers can deposit money and receive a higher interest rate than that available from banks.[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:Zhang-18):33 It soon became China's largest online money market fund and prompted competitors like Baidu and Tencent to introduce alternatives.[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:Zhang-18):33 Alibaba (the parent company of Alipay) reported having 152 million Yu'e Bao users in mid-2016, with 810 billion RMB (US$117 billion) in funds under management.[[19]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-19)

In 2015, Alipay's parent company was re-branded as [Ant Financial Services Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ant_Financial_Services_Group "Ant Financial Services Group").[[20]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-20)

In 2017, Alipay unveiled their [facial recognition](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Facial_recognition_system "Facial recognition system") payment service.[[21]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-21)

In 2020, Alipay upgraded from a payment financial instrument to an open platform for digital life.[[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-22)

In 2021, the mandate by the [Ministry of Industry and Information Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Industry_and_Information_Technology "Ministry of Industry and Information Technology") (MIIT) to open up the "[walled garden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Closed_platform "Closed platform")" ecosystems of the major tech companies has led to the introduction of interoperability of payment QR codes of Alipay and competing [WeChat Pay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WeChat_Pay "WeChat Pay") and [UnionPay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UnionPay "UnionPay")'s [Cloud QuickPass](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cloud_QuickPass "Cloud QuickPass") platforms.[[23]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-23)

In response to the increase in Alipay's payment volume due to use on Alibaba's e-commerce sites and others, Chinese regulators introduced new rules in 2020. The new rules focused on Alipay because the payment volume exploded due to its use on Alibaba's e-commerce sites and other platforms. By the second quarter in 2020, Alipay held 55.6% of China's third party mobile payment market. The People's Bank of China made rules that required payment firms to place money with regulators and anti-monopoly reviews would be triggered if the amount exceeded 50% market share.[[24]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-24)[[25]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-25)The rules included that the People's Bank of China mandate an online-payment clearing route through the NetsUnion Clearing Corporation, a centralized, state-overseen clearing body, and that unused consumer funds be held by a third-party payment provider in a non-interest-bearing account. These measures increased transparency and reduced systemic risk. [[26]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-26)

When Alipay operates outside of China, it must comply with local financial regulations, which may treat specific functions such as money-market funds or investment-linked products. In Singapore, such services may require prior authorization from securities or financial-services regulators before they can be offered to residents.[[27]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-27)

[![Image 2](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/33/Alipay_payment_20160121.jpg/330px-Alipay_payment_20160121.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Alipay_payment_20160121.jpg)

Food ordering with Alipay in mainland China

Alipay states that it operates with more than 65 financial institutions including [Visa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visa_Inc. "Visa Inc.") and [MasterCard](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MasterCard "MasterCard")[[28]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-Alipay-28) to provide payment services for [Taobao](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taobao "Taobao") and [Tmall](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tmall "Tmall") as well as more than 460,000 online and local Chinese businesses.

Alipay is used in smartphones with their Alipay Wallet app. [QR code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/QR_code "QR code") payment codes are used for local in-store payments.[[29]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-29) The Alipay app also provides features such as [credit card](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Credit_card "Credit card") bill payments, [bank account](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bank_account "Bank account") managements, [P2P transfer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peer-to-peer_banking "Peer-to-peer banking"), [prepay mobile phone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prepay_mobile_phone "Prepay mobile phone") top-up, bus and train ticket purchases, food orders, [vehicles for hire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vehicle_for_hire "Vehicle for hire"), insurance selections and a digital identification document storage.[[30]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-30) Alipay also allows online check-out on most Chinese-based websites such as [Taobao](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taobao "Taobao") and [Tmall](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tmall "Tmall").[[31]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-31)

The Alipay app allows users to add their own services provided from different companies to create a more personalised experience.[[32]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-32)

Since late 2008, Alipay has promoted public service payment services and has covered more than 300 cities nationwide, supporting more than 1,200 partner organizations.[[33]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-33) In addition to utility bills such as water and electricity, Alipay also extends their services to areas such as paying transportation fines, property fees, and cable television fees.[[34]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-34) Common online payment services also include [hydropower](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hydropower "Hydropower") coal payment, tuition payment and traffic fine.[[35]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-35)

On 15 January 2009, Alipay launched a [credit card](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Credit_card "Credit card") repayment service, supporting 39 domestic bank-issued credit cards.[[36]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-36) It is currently the most popular third-party repayment platform. The main advantages are free credit card bills checking, repayments with no administrative fee, as well as automatic repayment, repayment reminders and other value-added services.[[37]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-37) In the first quarter of 2014, 76% of credit cards were also paid by Alipay Wallet.

From December 2013, several chain convenience store companies, including Meiyijia, Hongqi Chain, and Qishiduo C-STORE and [7-Eleven](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/7-Eleven "7-Eleven"), have successively supported Alipay payment; in December, Beijing taxi drivers began to accept Alipay to pay the fare. Subsequently, [Wanda Cinema](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wanda_Cinemas "Wanda Cinemas"), Joy City, [Wangfujing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wangfujing "Wangfujing") and other large-scale retail companies as well as movie theaters, KTV, and catering companies have access to Alipay. From 26 March 2019, the service fee will be charged for the payment of credit card through Alipay. Customers only pay the portion of the payment that exceeds 2,000 yuan at 0.1%.[[38]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-38) In addition to this, in 2019, Walgreens accepted Alipay as payment in 3,000 US stores. Walgreen's products are available to Chinese customers through Alibaba's Tmall online marketplace.[[39]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-39) The payment application can also be used on Alibaba.com's site and Taobao as a means of payment. A Nielsen report suggests that over 90% of Chinese tourists would be willing to use mobile payment overseas if given the option.[[40]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-40) Many Chinese tourists do not have international credit cards, and so Alipay is a payment option. Digital payments have become the norm in China as the government pushes a cashless system even in rural and village areas.[[41]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-41)

In November 2019, Alipay introduced Tourpass,[[42]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-42) a service component that allows non-Chinese users to use its mobile payment feature by pre-loading Chinese Yuan equivalent foreign currency into the app.[[43]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-43)

In 2020, Alipay used a QR code system to help in containing the [COVID-19 outbreak](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic"). The health code system tags users one of three colors according to their location, basic health information and travel history.[[44]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-44)

"Beauty filters" were included to Alipay's face-scan payment system[[45]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-TelpoAlipay711-45) in a new upgrade that was released in July 2019. The market has responded well to the "beauty filters," which make users seem better when they use the program to make payments.[[46]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-46)[Alipay Tap](https://www.alipayplus.com/nfc/) is a payment function launched by Alipay in July 2024. Alipay+ NFC[[47]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-47) enables wallets to offer tap-to-pay acceptance across Mastercard's global contactless network, all within your existing wallet infrastructure.

Outside of China, more than 300 worldwide merchants use Alipay to sell directly to consumers in China.[[48]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-48) It currently[_[as of?](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual\_of\_Style/Dates\_and\_numbers#Chronological\_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")_] supports transactions in 18 foreign currencies.

Since the launch of Alipay in the [Mainland China](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mainland_China "Mainland China"), Ant Financial introduced a series of expansion of the services to other countries.[[49]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-49)[[50]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-50)[[51]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-51)[[52]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-52) Other than expanding into individual countries, the system would also be integrated with online payment platform providers. [Ant Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ant_Group "Ant Group") had acquired a majority stake into 2C2P, a Singapore-based provider used by merchants worldwide in April 2022, and would eventually integrate Alipay with 2C2P.[[53]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-53)

In 2018, Alipay bought 20% shares in Bangladeshi mobile financial service provider [bKash Limited](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BKash "BKash").[[54]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-54)

In 2017, Ant Financial expanded to Hong Kong. In a joint venture with [CK Hutchison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CK_Hutchison_Holdings "CK Hutchison Holdings"), as Alipay Payment Services (HK), it launched the "AlipayHK" brand.[[55]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-55) A standalone app provides features such as mobile payments and P2P transfers. All transactions are made and settled in local [Hong Kong dollars](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong_dollar "Hong Kong dollar").[[56]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-56) The service then became available in major chain stores including [McDonald's](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/McDonald's "McDonald's"), [7-Eleven](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/7-Eleven "7-Eleven") and [Circle K](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Circle_K "Circle K").[[57]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-57)[Wet markets](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wet_market "Wet market") and other merchants were also supported.[[58]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-58) From 2020 and 2021, with AlipayHK passing two million users, the service became accepted on [MTR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MTR "MTR"), [buses](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kowloon_Motor_Bus "Kowloon Motor Bus") and [ferries](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hong_Kong_&_Kowloon_Ferry "Hong Kong & Kowloon Ferry").[[59]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-59)

Alipay entered Japan in 2015, with network up to 38,000 vendors. Ant Financial hopes that their network in Japan could help Chinese tourists that are heading to Japan.[[60]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-60)

Alipay was introduced to the Philippines in 2018 by [Asia United Bank](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asia_United_Bank "Asia United Bank") (AUB).[[61]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-61) AUB also introduced [WeChat Pay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WeChat_Pay "WeChat Pay"), and intends to use both payment systems to cater to Chinese tourists visiting the country.[[62]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-62)

Ant Financial also has invested on Mynt, the operator of the mobile service [GCash](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GCash "GCash") which caters to the Philippine market. It helped convert GCash into a cashless mobile payment service similar to Alipay.[[63]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-63)

In 2017, Ant Financial partnered with CC Financial, a start-up company in Singapore. Alipay plans to expand its 20,000 acceptance points in Singapore, and open up their platform to Singapore banking users.[[64]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-64)[[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-65)

Alipay was introduced in South Korea in 2015, and is now available at various merchants around the country. Users can even receive an instant tax refund at four major airports in the country. In 2019, taxis in [Seoul](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Seoul "Seoul") and Starbucks will accept Alipay via Kakaopay.[[66]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-66)[[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-67)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-68)

Alipay entered Vietnam in 2010, Ant Financial indicated that their network in Vietnam could assist Chinese tourists visiting the country.[[69]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-69) Currently in Vietnam, [Davitrans](https://davitrans.com/), a proxy shipping service, is the only website in the country that accepts Alipay as one of its payment methods.

In February 2019, Alipay and Tourism Australia announced a service to promote Australian destinations to Chinese tourists using the city of [Sydney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sydney "Sydney") as a 12-month pilot project. The new Sydney City Card will introduce an interactive city map in the Alipay app to alert tourists to participating locations and retailers that welcome Alipay payments. A similar initiative will be trialed concurrently in [Queenstown, New Zealand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queenstown,_New_Zealand "Queenstown, New Zealand").[[70]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-70)

Ant Financial has partnered with [First Data](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/First_Data "First Data") in 2017.[[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-71) It allows Alipay service to be used at point-of-sale with more than four million retail partners in United States.[[72]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-72)

In 2017, Alipay partnered with SnapPay[[73]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-73) to allow Canadian retailers to accept Chinese currency from Chinese shoppers. As of 2019, there are 800 merchants in Canada that supports Alipay, including most [Cadillac Fairview](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cadillac_Fairview "Cadillac Fairview") mall locations in partnership with OTT Pay,[[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-74) such as the flagship [Eaton Centre](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eaton_Centre "Eaton Centre") of Toronto and [CF Chinook Centre](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CF_Chinook_Centre "CF Chinook Centre") in Calgary.[[75]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-75)[[76]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-76) Air Canada began allowing transactions in Alipay to book flights from Canada and the United States beginning in August 2018, after initially rolling out the feature for booking of flights originating from China.[[77]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-alipaycanada-77)

In July 2021, Alipay was introduced in Brazil as a digital wallet for transactions on AliExpress. With this feature, customers could store balance in their account, collect bonuses and use it as a form of payment for purchases made on the marketplace,[[78]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-78) but the Alipay Brazil Digital Consumer Account was discontinued on 30 September 2022.

Alipay partners with Silkpay[[79]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-79) to enable merchants in France and other European countries to accept Alipay payments online and in-store.[[80]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-80)

Alipay has partnered with Splitit and Epassi in Iceland.[[81]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-81)[[82]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-82)

Alipay partnered with [UniCredit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UniCredit "UniCredit"), [SIA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SIA_S.p.A. "SIA S.p.A.") and [Banca Sella Group](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Banca_Sella_Group "Banca Sella Group") to enable app payments in physical and online stores in Italy.[[83]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-83)[[84]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-84)

Alipay has started cooperating with Vipps in Norway. 30 shops in Bergen are ready to receive Alipay-customers, and in January 2019 some shops in Oslo will be ready.[[85]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-85)

Alipay proposed a partnership with [Sberbank](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sberbank "Sberbank") to provide immediate money transfers by cellphone number between Russia and China by 2022: the proposal pre-dates the [Russian invasion of Ukraine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2022_Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine "2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine") in 2022, which resulted in [Visa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visa_Inc. "Visa Inc.") and [Mastercard](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mastercard "Mastercard") suspending operations in Russia.[[86]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-86)

Alipay partnered with Barclaycard in United Kingdom, bringing Alipay to UK retailers.[[87]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-87)

## Comparison with other payment systems

[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Alipay&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Comparison with other payment systems")]

Alipay is conceptually similar to [Apple Pay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Pay "Apple Pay"), [WeChat Pay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WeChat_Pay "WeChat Pay") and [PayPal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PayPal "PayPal") because it overlays traditional card payment methods. Although users receive immediate notification of the transaction, the main difference among Alipay and an [instant payment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Instant_payment "Instant payment") system, like [Venmo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Venmo "Venmo") or [Zelle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zelle_(payment_service) "Zelle (payment service)") is that the funds transfer between counterparties is not immediate.[[88]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-88) The settlement time depends on the payment method chosen by the customer, while for instant payment systems, the funds are transferred within seconds or minutes.

At the time it was implemented and integrated into Taobao, the mandatory escrow feature of Alipay was a major institutional innovation for e-commerce platforms.[[89]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:Liu-89):53 This was a major reason Taobao was able to outcompete eBay/EachNet in the Chinese market.[[89]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:Liu-89):53–54

Prior to Alipay, third-party payment was a legal grey area in China.[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:Zhang-18):32 In an effort to build trust with regulators, Alibaba informed regulators about the development of Alipay from the outset and provided regulators with monthly updates on its development.[[89]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:Liu-89):55 Alipay's development has been largely guided by regulatory policies including frequent disclosure. Instead of launching payments in secret, Alibaba chose to keep regulators informed and up to date on progress which helped to reduce uncertainty around a quick growing payment platform. This approach made difference because Alipay was growing and soon becoming something that can be used as a checkout tool. It was evolving into a major financial infrastructure layer that that was used for settlements. One of the questions that arose in regards to Alipay was whether it was a bank or not because it had a giant pool of investments.[[90]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-90)[[91]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-91) As Alipay's popularity increased, it became accepted by Chinese regulators.[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:Zhang-18):32–33 In 2010, the [People's Bank of China](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/People's_Bank_of_China "People's Bank of China") (PBoC) issued administrative measures regarding non-financial payment services.[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:Zhang-18):33 These measures retroactively recognized the legal status of online third-party payment platforms like Alipay.[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:Zhang-18):33 In 2011, Alipay obtained a license to become one of the first licensed non-financial institutions to conduct payment operations.[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:Zhang-18):33

The PBoC supported the growth of Yu'e Bao and similar funds with a permissive regulatory environment initially but began increasing regulation of such funds in 2017.[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-:Zhang-18):33

In September 2021, the [Chinese government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chinese_government "Chinese government") stated its intent to dismantle the Alipay super app into separate businesses due to concerns over "systemic financial risks" as Chinese e-commerce and fintech firms had begun to amass huge troves of user data for their operations. The move essentially split [Ant Group's](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ant_Group "Ant Group") consumer lending businesses, credit card-like Huabei and micro-loan provider Jiebei, from Alipay's financial offerings.[[92]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-92)[[93]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-93)[[94]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-94)

In November 2021, Alipay introduced a new privacy protection feature amid growing concerns over data privacy and after China's Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL) came into effect on 1 November 2021. The PIPL prohibits the unnecessary collection of personal information, abuse of personal privacy, and data exchanges with overseas entities. Alipay's new feature allows users to track how the app collects data about them and is an interactive equivalent to a [privacy policy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Privacy_policy "Privacy policy"), which explains how an app collects, stores, and shares user data.[[95]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-95)

Alipay was banned in India (along with other Chinese apps) on 2 September 2020 by the government amid the [2020 China-India skirmish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2020_China-India_skirmish "2020 China-India skirmish").[[96]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-96)[[97]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alipay#cite_note-97)
